Annotation.String: Loan Contract

Loan Contract is a contractual agreement between lenders and borrowers, which is based on a borrowerâ€™s request for a loan and includes, for example, loan type, borrower(s), and general terms and conditions.
Comment: A BankAccount is a bank account associated with the bank account contract.
Integrity Condition:StartDate of TermSpecificationValidityPeriod, TermAgreementFixingPeriod, and IncomingPaymentAgreementPeriod have to be the same date.
Constraint: Loan Contract Create - During creation of a loan contract , it is required to specify the details of Term Agreement and Inpayment Agreement.
An universal unique identifier of a bank account contract.
GDT: NOSC_UUID
An identifier of a bank account contract.
GDT: BankAccountContractID
A CurrencyCode specifies the currency of the bank account contract.
GDT: CurrencyCode
A BankAccount is a bank account associated with the bank account contract.
BOCT: BankAccountIdentifyingElements
A coded representation of the life cycle status of a bank account contract.
GDT: BankAccountContractLifeCycleStatusCode
Name of LifeCycleStatusCode.
GDT: LANGUAGEINDEPENDENT_MEDIUM_Name
A BorrowerParty is a party who takes up a financial instrument, in this case a loan contract.
A Product describes the (financial) product upon which the bank account contract is based.
A record of a business event that changes a bank account contract.
An OperationalBankContractManagement is responsible for managing operational bank contracts and executing the related banking processes.
A functional unit that is responsible for managing the contract.
Business Process Chain Assignment is the assignment of a business object node to a business process chain.
BOCT: BusProcChnAssgmtFSElmnts
A ChangeStateID is a unique identifier for a change state.
GDT: ChangeStateID
A ClearingBankAccountContract is a BankAccountContract that receives incoming payments that have to be transferred to a specific loan contract.
Remark: The ClearingBankAccountContract can be changed.
A specification of term elements that affects all term agreements of the loan contract.
Corresponds to tab 'Agreement' in backend Agreement number not included because only one agreement is allowed

PropertyValue.String: Refer to API Loan Disbursement Requests for supported query options
Refer to API Loan Payment Skip Requests for supported query options

Annotation.String: Is an amount with the corresponding currency unit.
Value of an amount.
Currency unit in accordance with the ISO 4217 three-character code.
A currency must always be specified. GDT: CurrencyCode
Business Process Chain Assignment

Is the assignment of a business object node to a business process chain. /nDefault: If BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode is not specified, code value "400006 - Adhoc Loan Contract Change" will be used by default.
UUID of the business process chain to which the hosting business object is assigned to.
GDT: UUID
BDT: BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode
Name of BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode
GDT: EXTENDED_Name
Bank Account Identifying Elements
The identifying elements of a bank account supported by bank account management in Financial Services (Banking).

Bank account number (Basic Bank Account Number, BBAN). An account number that is assigned by the account managing bank. It uniquely identifies a bank account in most countries only together with the bank.
GDT: BankAccountID
International bank account number (International Bank Account Number, IBAN).
GDT: BankAccountStandardID
BankAccountInternalID is a proprietary identifier for a bank account.
GDT: BankAccountInternalID
A UUID is an universal unique identifier of a bank account.
GDT: NOSC_UUID
CurrencyCode is a coded representation of the currency of the bank account.
GDT: CurrencyCode
Bank where the account is held.
A bank is a business entity that performs financial investment services and payment transactions.
Bank Identification Code (BIC) of the Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunications (S.W.I.F.T.), see GDT BankStandardID.
GDT: BankStandardID
Number of the bank in a clearing system (see GDT BankRoutingID).
GDT: BankRoutingID

Country in which the bank identified earlier goes about its business. If the bank is a member in a national clearing system, the country to which this clearing system belongs is entered here. Constraint: Only listID 3166-1 of listAgencyID 5 will be supported.
GDT: CountryCode
A Product describes the (financial) product upon which the bank account contract is based.
A BankAccountContractProductionControl defines the parameters that control the processing and handling in the production of a bank account contract.
Comment: BankAccountContractProductionControl defines the contract information that controls the production of a bank account contract.
Bank Account Contract Production Control
A BankAccountContractProductionControl defines the parameters that control the processing and handling in the production of a bank account contract.
ID is an identifier of a bank account production control.
Filter: Only 'Eq' is supported as logical operator. GDT: BankAccountContractProductionControlID
A description of the Bank Account Contract Production Control.
GDT: LANGUAGEINDEPENDENT_MEDIUM_Description
Business Event Record
A record of a business event that changes a bank account contract.
Date when the bank account contract becomes operational from the customer's point of view.
GDT: Date
Date when a bank account contract is actually canceled.
GDT: Date
Date when a cancellation of a bank account contract is planned.
GDT: Date
Operational Bank Contract Management
A functional unit that is responsible for managing the contract.
A unique identifier of an organizational unit that is responsible for managing operational bank contracts and executing the related banking processes.
Constraint: The ID may not exceed the length of 8 and may only consist of numerical characters. GDT: OrganisationalCentreID
A name of the Operational Bank Contract Management.
GDT: MEDIUM_Name
A description of the Operational Bank Contract Management.
GDT: MEDIUM_Description
An identifier of a bank account contract. GDT: BankAccountContractID
Content of BankAccountContractID.
SchemeID of the BankAccountContractID.
Default: If filter value is not provided, BAC.001 will be used as default value for filter.
SchemeAgencyID of the BankAccountContractID.
Default: If filter value is not provided, 310 will be used as default value for filter.
Party Identifying Elements

PartyIdentifyingElements does contain all identifying elements of a party (business partner). Remark: PartyTypeCode is value 147 (Business Partner). Integrity Condition: Either PartyUUID or PartyID must be specified during creation.
The UUID of a party (business partner).
GDT: UUID
The ID of a party (business partner).
GDT: PartyID
Bank Account
An account that holds funds in a bank and is subject to deposits and withdrawals.
A name of the account holder of a bank account.
GDT: BankAccountHolderName_V1

Bank account number (Basic Bank Account Number, BBAN). An account number that is assigned by the account managing bank. It uniquely identifies a bank account in most countries only together with the bank
GDT: BankAccountID
International bank account number (International Bank Account Number, IBAN)
GDT: BankAccountStandardID
CurrencyCode is a coded representation of the currency of the bank account.
GDT: CurrencyCode
Bank where the account is held.
Direct Debit Mandate
A identifier of Direct Debit Mandate
GDT: DirectDebitMandateID
A unique identifier of a creditor party of direct debit mandate.
GDT: PartyID
System Administrative Data
SystemAdministrativeData is administrative data that is stored in a system. This data includes system users and change dates/times.
Creation date/time (date and time stamp).
GDT: GLOBAL_DateTime
Time (date and time stamp) of last change.
GDT: GLOBAL_DateTime

"UPPEROPEN_DatePeriod is a period that is defined by two points in time.These points in time are expressed in calendar days. UPPEROPEN_DatePeriod includes the start time-point, but excludes the end time-point."
The StartDate is the start date of the period
GDT: Date Constraint for Create/Update: If StartDate and EndDate are provided no Duration must be provided.
The duration of the period.

GDT: Duration Constraint: Allowed literals for Duration are: Y = Duration in years, M = Duration in months, D = Duration in days. Constraint for Create/Update: If duration is provided StartDate must be provided and EndDate must not be provided.
The EndDate is the end date of period
GDT: Date

"RescissionPeriod is a period that is defined by two points in time.These points in time are expressed in calendar days. RescissionPeriod includes the start time-point, but excludes the end time-point."
The StartDate is the start date of the rescission period
GDT: Date Constraint for Create/Update: If StartDate and EndDate are provided no Duration must be provided.
The duration of the rescission period.

GDT: Duration Constraint: Allowed literals for Duration are: Y = Duration in years, M = Duration in months, D = Duration in days. Constraint for Create/Update: If duration is provided StartDate must be provided and EndDate must not be provided.
The EndDate is the end date of rescission period
A period in which the committed capital adjustment is valid
The StartDate is the start date of the commitment capital adjustment validity period
GDT: Date
The EndDate is the end date of the commitment capital adjustment validity period.
GDT: Date
Calculation Period
The period for which EffectiveYieldPercent is calculated.
The StartDate is the start date of the Calculation Period.
GDT: Date
The EndDate is the end date of the Calculation Period.
GDT: Date
A specification of term elements that affects all term agreements of the loan contract.
Specifies the period in which the specification for the loan contract term is valid.
Indicates whether the term specification is active.
GDT: IndicatorRemark: If ActiveIndicator is false the term specification is preliminary. If ActiveIndicator is true the term specification is Active. The default value for this indicator is FALSE.
Specifies the period in which the borrower has the right to rescind the term specification related to the loan contract.

A ClearingBankAccountContract is a BankAccountContract that receives incoming payments that have to be transferred to a specific loan contract.Remark: The ClearingBankAccountContract can be changed.Constraint: Loan Contract Create - During creation of a Loan Contract , it is required to either specify the ClearingBankAccountContractUUID or ClearingBankAccountContract LoanContractUsedIndicator.
A UUID is an universal unique identifier of a bank account contract.
GDT: NOSC_UUID
Indicates whether the loan contract is used as clearing bank account contract

GDT: Indicator Remark: If this indicator is not set, it implies that the Clearing Bank Account Contract used for incoming payments to the loan , is different than the loan contract. Constraint : If LoanContractUsedIndicator is true, the ClearingBankAccountContractUUID must not be specified

The TermAgreement is an agreement on the terms of a loan contractCorresponds to tab 'Condition Fixing' in backend Constraint: Only context code value 34 "Loan Contract Term Agreement Condition Determination Key" is supported for BankContractFinancialConditionContextValueCode = VC or BankContractFinancialConditionContextValueCode = FC. Note: While creating LoanContract we have to pass same attributes for all term agreements.It means all the records in TermAgreement should have the same attributes during POST call.Even if some attributes are not required, we can pass those with blank value to make the request homogeneous.
An identifier of a term agreement belonging to a loan contract.
GDT: BankAccountContractTermAgreementID
The CommittedAmount is the committed amount of the term agreement of a loan contract.
GDT: Amount
A coded representation of the value of a context.
GDT: BankContractFinancialConditionContextValueCode
Name of BankContractFinancialConditionContextCode
GDT: LONG_Name
A FixingPeriod is the period for which the term agreement is fixed.
Use: the FixingPeriod is the period for which the committed capital amount is fixed.
An Incoming Payment Agreement is an agreement that specifies the term for the incoming payments of the loan contract.
An identifier of an incoming payment agreement belonging to a loan contract.
GDT: BankAccountContractIncomingPaymentAgreementID
Is the period in which the IncomingPaymentAgreement is valid.
The amount of the final payment that is planned for the incoming payment agreement.
Use: Use: The planned final payment for a loan is a payment agreed with the borrower party. It is due as the last payment of the loan. GDT: Amount
Indicates whether the last payment date in the payment plan must be adjusted to the end date of the period of the incoming payment agreement or not.
GDT: Indicator

A payment transaction instruction is an instruction for payment transactions related to the bank account contract. Remark: A payment transaction instruction is used in banking business transaction to trigger a payment transaction.
A UUID is a globally unique identifier for a PaymentTransactionInstruction.
GDT: NOSC_UUID
A BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode is a coded representation of the type of business transaction from a banking view.
Remark: Specifies the business transaction for which the payment transaction instruction is used. GDT: BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode
Name of BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode.
GDT: MEDIUM_Name
A BankingBusinessTransactionProcessingResultingPaymentTypeCode is a coded representation of the payment type that results from processing a banking business transaction.
GDT: BankingBusinessTransactionProcessingResultPaymentTypeCode
Name of BankingBusinessTransactionProcessingResultPaymentTypeCode.
GDT: MEDIUM_Name
A BankContractPaymentTransactionInstructionTypeCode is a coded representation of the type of a bank contract payment transaction instruction.
GDT: BankContractPaymentTransactionInstructionTypeCode
Name of BankContractPaymentTransactionInstructionTypeCode.
GDT: MEDIUM_Name
A coded representation of the credit or debit side of payment transaction instruction.
Remark: A DebitCreditCode is used to identify the resulting payment transaction as a credit or debit transaction. GDT: DebitCreditCode
Name of DebitCreditCode.
GDT: MEDIUM_Name
Contains the bank account to which funds are either transferred or collected from.

Comment: The Counterparty is the party that participates in a payment transaction. Remark: If DebitCreditCode is 'Credit', funds are transferred to the bank account. If DebitCreditCode is 'Debit', funds are collected from the bank account.
The direct debit mandate used in a payment transaction instruction.
Remark: DirectDebitMandate is only applicable, if value for DebitCreditCode is 'Debit'.
Indicates that PaymentTransactionInstruction is mappable.
GDT: Indicator

Adjusts the loan capital amount that is committed for the specified period.Remark: This functionality is realized in the backend using Seasonal Limits.The effects of this adjustment can be seen using transaction BCA_CN_KFG and requesting "Interest and Capital Amounts".Constraint: Increase adjustments can be made in the contract future; decrease adjustments can only be made in the contract present.
Unique identifier of an adjustment of a committed capital for a loan contract.
GDT: CommittedCapitalAdjustmentID
The period in which the Committed Capital Adjustment is valid.
It describes the period when the committed capital adjustment is used. GDT:ValidityPeriod
The amount by which the committed capital is adjusted.
A negative amount decreases the committed capital amount and a positive amount increases the committed capital amount. GDT: Amount
A BalanceKeyFigure is a key figure that describes an amount pertaining to a loan contract.
A BankAccountContractBalanceKeyFigureCode is a coded representation of a key figure for a balance of a BankAccountContract.
Remark: The valid code values for a loan contract are described in the BDT BankAccountContractBalanceKeyFigureCode.
Name of BankAccountContractBalanceKeyFigureCode.
Description of a balance key figure.

PropertyValue.String: An amount that is the initial loan contract amount.

Annotation.String: An amount that specifies the amount of the balance key figure.
Integrity constraint: CurrencyCode of Amount has to be equal to CurrencyCode of LoanContract.
An EffectiveYields is the effective yield (effective interest) of a bank account contract.
A coded representation of the reason for a effective yield calculation for a bank account contract.
Remark: The valid code values for a loan contract are described in the GDT EffectiveYieldCalculationReasonCode.
The name of an effective yield calculation reason code.
A coded representation of the method for calculating the effective interest rate.
Remark: The valid code values for a loan contract are described in the GDT EffectiveYieldCalculationMethodCode.
The name of a calculation method code.
The effective yield percent.
BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode is the coded representation of the banking business transaction which is associated with the calculation of the bank account contract effective yield.
Remark: GDT: BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode
Name of BankingBusinessTransactionTypeCode.
GDT: MEDIUM_Name
The date at which the effective yield validity begins.
The period for which EffectiveYieldPercent is calculated.
It describes the period for which EffectiveYieldPercent is calculated.GDT:CLOSED_DatePeriod
